mechanism_narrative: A rising level of nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding aggregate employment income, which supports corporate top-line growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns follow rising PAYEMS in less than 52% of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5%, restrictive monetary policy raises the cost of capital sufficiently to compress equity valuations, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered episodes over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between positive industrial production growth and a declining federal funds rate reflects an accommodative monetary environment supporting manufacturing expansion, which predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term between INDPRO and FEDFUNDS shows a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window or a t-statistic below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of high consumer price inflation, month-over-month increases in retail sales signal demand-driven margin compression and impending monetary tightening, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances when RSAFS rises during high inflation periods over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 600 thousand units signal a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and durable goods, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of episodes when HOUST is below 600 over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll employment declines month-over-month, the resulting negative income shock reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns follow month-over-month PAYEMS declines in less than 55% of instances over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The interaction between the federal funds rate and the consumer price index captures the real policy rate; when FEDFUNDS rises while CPIAUCSL is stable, the increasing real rate tightens financial conditions and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction term fails to produce a Rank IC above 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Negative quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth signals a recessionary contraction in aggregate demand that reduces corporate revenue across sectors, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns follow negative GDPC1 growth in more than 45% of quarters over any 10-year rolling window.
